title: sorbus aria subsp. longifolia
intro: Sorbus aria subsp. longifolia, also known as the Whitebeam, is a subspecies of tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its broad, elliptical leaves and white flowers. The Whitebeam has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sorbus aria subsp. longifolia include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
